program.name: ASX Graduate Program
program.duration_text: You will complete an 18-month tailored program designed to give you cross-functional experience.
program.rotation_text: You will complete three six-month rotations, tailored to your career pathway.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or an Australian or New Zealand permanent resident.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the last two years prior to the program start date.
disciplines: Technology pathway: information systems, computer science, engineering, economics, data science, or business management.
Business/Commercial pathway: business/commerce, management, economics, behavioural science, communication, environmental studies, and other degree types.
Corporate Finance pathway: accounting, actuarial studies, finance, or another degree with a strong financial or analytical focus.
minimum_requirements: You must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or an Australian or New Zealand permanent resident.
You must have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the last two years prior to the program start date.
You must be available and eligible to commence the role in ASX’s Sydney head office in March 2026.

stages.name: Online application form
stages.details: Complete an online application form and provide supporting documents such as a resume, academic transcript, and proof of citizenship.

stages.name: Online assessment
stages.details: Complete a short online assessment and record a video response to questions about you and your motivation for the program.

stages.name: Virtual Assessment Centre
stages.details: Attend a virtual assessment centre with group activities and a short interview.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: If successful, People and Culture will call shortly after the virtual assessment centre to make an employment offer.

tips: Try to complete the online application form in one sitting and have your supporting documents ready (for example, your resume, academic transcript, and proof of citizenship).
Do the online assessment in a quiet place where you will not be disturbed.
At the assessment centre, show how you work in a team by engaging with your group and letting your personality come through.

notes: Application opening and closing dates, streams (pathways), program duration, rotation structure, and Sydney location are stated on the ASX Graduate Program page.
Work rights, graduation window, and March 2026 start location requirement are stated under eligibility requirements on the ASX Graduate Program page.
Suggested study areas are listed under each pathway section on the same page.
Recruitment stages, timing window (September and October), and the assessment centre date are described on the ASX Graduate Program page, including tips for each stage.
I found the official ASX ‘Reports’ repository page listing Annual Reports (including FY24 and FY25) and other shareholder reports, plus official Sustainability pages and the Media Centre index.

However, the tool could not fetch the FY24 and FY25 annual report PDFs from asx.com.au (the requests failed with an internal fetch error), so I could not reliably extract executive themes, profit engine, headwinds, ESG summary, or recent strategic pivots from the official reports/transcripts.
Because the requested fields must come from official company reports/transcripts/pages, and the most relevant reports were not accessible in this run, I am returning nulls rather than guessing.
